Enron's New Chief Executive Is a Former Enron Employee

04/04/2001
Reuters English News Service
(C) Reuters Limited 2001.

NEW YORK, April 4 (Reuters) - Enron Corp's new chief executive, Jeff Skilling, was a former Enron employee, the company said on Monday. 
Skilling, who joined Enron in January, was a vice president of Enron North America from 1999 to 2001. 
Enron said it would not comment further on Skilling's employment history. 
Enron shares fell $1.50 to $13.50 in New York trading. 
Enron's shares have fallen more than 50 percent since the company reported third-quarter earnings last week. 
The company said it had lost $1.1 billion in the third quarter, compared with a loss of $1.2 billion in the same period a year earlier. 
Enron said it had $1.1 billion in cash and cash equivalents as of March 31.
